prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
The beasts of the digital sky have wept silver, and our chief wizard hath declared the third quarter blessed with an astronomical twenty-seven percent growth in recurring revenue, as the tomes of profit and loss mystically glow in the castle of server farms beyond the realm of visible sky.

Behold, the customer acquisition cost hath shrunk like the shrew in the potionless cauldron, reaching a paltry two hundred and twenty-eight gold ducats per new soul bound by service, while the churn rate remained stable at the mercy of only two percent of our enchanted users fleeing for lesser magical realms.

The chief wizards have assembled in the great hall of coin to decree that marketing cloud expenditures have bloomed to a mighty thirty-four percent of all revenue, a testament to our endless belief in the power of whispered promises through the aether of false hope.

Verily, the company doth loom large in the land of software, now valued at thirteen and sevenfold times its annual proceeds, as the market of future wonders hath bestowed upon us a blessèd cap of four hundred and three million castles' worth of silver without harvesting a single crop or hammering a single nail.
